next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 2

The Bebop had a stretch of time that was not night anymore and not yet morning either, a slack ugly hour when the pipes ticked in the walls, the air recycler breathed dust-dry air through old filters, and Jet's first pot of coffee always smelled a little burned because the burner ran hot on the left side. Ed liked that hour. Adults were slow in it. Systems were not.

Ed had been awake through most of it, or maybe all of it. Sleep had happened at some point on the couch with Ein against Ed's ribs, but the ship's ID anomaly had kept making a scratchy little noise in Ed's head, so Ed had rolled off the cushion before the lights fully changed and crawled back to the main console. The dark ship in their docking spot was still clean on the public registries. The registration was clean. The tax records were clean. The maintenance history was clean. It was too clean, as if somebody had taken a rag to the fingerprints.

Ed liked fingerprints.

Three archive windows hung over the main screen in layers: bounty board, old freight liens, and a deep crawler that smelled in Ed's mind like old cardboard and forgotten passwords. The ship's handshake pattern was still there under the public ID, a tiny wrong rhythm buried in the outgoing ping. It was not illegal, and it was not even hidden very well. It was just old, old enough that the modern systems treated it like dust.

Ed loved dust too.

"Aha-ha," Ed said softly to the console. "Crunchy little signal biscuit. Ed bites, biscuit breaks, inside is another biscuit."

Ein lifted his head from the floor, watched Ed for a moment,...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Bebop arrives at Mama Yun's to find their spot taken by an unfamiliar ship
Establish crew dynamic through argument about whose fault the failed Deimos bounty was
state_deltas_required: Territorial irritation compounded by poverty and a failed bounty — the crew is broke, hungry, and someone has taken the one reliable thing Jet has
Routine return becomes territorial dispute with a hidden edge; the crew unites around petty grievance unaware the ship is bait; Ed detects something anomalous in the ship's signal
